pi_items.source: Task description and setup_task.sh both reference this style name. Following GeoServer naming conventions, this is a reasonable and properly formatted SLD style name.

setup_geoserver.sh lines 120-122 show the layer 'ne_populated_places' being published into the 'ne' workspace, which makes its full layer name 'ne:ne_populated_places' in GeoServer notation. Verified against GeoServer REST API conventions.

GeoServer documentation (https://docs.geoserver.org/latest/en/user/styling/ysld/reference/transforms.html) confirms PointStacker uses cellSize parameter for point aggregation. Task specifies cellSize=60 pixels. While documentation shows examples with cellSize values of 30 and 100, cellSize is a configurable parameter with no single 'correct' value - 60 pixels is a reasonable intermediate value for typical map clustering.
Verified via color-hex.com and other color reference sources. #0000FF is standard pure blue (RGB 0,0,255). This is the correct hex notation for blue color used in SLD styling.
Verified via color-hex.com and other color reference sources. #FF0000 is standard pure red (RGB 255,0,0). This is the correct hex notation for red color used in SLD styling.

GeoServer official documentation (https://docs.geoserver.org/latest/en/user/styling/sld/extensions/rendering-transform.html and Boundless documentation) confirms gs:PointStacker is a real Vector-to-Vector rendering transformation function that aggregates dense point data into clusters. This is the correct function name for point clustering in GeoServer.

privileged_info_summary: The style_point_clustering task uses real data from Natural Earth's Populated Places dataset (ne_110m_populated_places), which is a publicly available, curated global dataset of cities and populated areas. All metadata claims are verified: the layer 'ne:ne_populated_places' is correctly named following GeoServer workspace:layer notation, the 'clustered_places' style name follows proper SLD naming conventions, the gs:PointStacker rendering transformation is a legitimate GeoServer function for server-side point clustering that outputs a 'count' attribute for conditional styling, and the cellSize value of 60 pixels is a reasonable parameter within documented ranges (30-100 pixels shown in examples). Color codes #0000FF (blue) and #FF0000 (red) are standard web-safe colors. The task requires conditional SLD styling rules to render clustered points (count>1) as blue circles with labels and single points (count=1) as red stars.
